Traditionally, Lepidocordia williamsii has been used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, respiratory issues, and gastrointestinal problems, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In herbal medicine, Lepidocordia williamsii is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used in the form of tinctures, capsules, and tea blends to leverage its health benefits.
